search_queries_used: site:gmc-uk.org decision making consent recording decisions capacity patient refuses treatment
site:rcem.ac.uk mental health toolkit patient wants to leave decline treatment capacity
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic detention, restraint or forced treatment
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Legal authority and emergency action depend on UAE law and clinician assessment.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Patients declining assessment, treatment, admission, transfer or follow-up; children, impaired capacity, emergencies and mental-health law require specific review.
UAE_applicability: Documentation principles transfer, but UAE consent, capacity, guardianship, mental-health and emergency-treatment law and facility policy govern.
recency_verification: Current GMC guidance and RCEM April 2023 toolkit were reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact GMC recording section supports documenting the patient’s concerns or goals, information exchange, decision, agreed or declined action, decision-maker, completed risk discussion and clinician plan; it does not override UAE law.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording the exchange of information leading to a decision, the decision and action agreed, who made or agreed it, and a decision to take no action.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact capacity section supports a focused, time- and decision-specific assessment only when completed; it does not auto-state capacity.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports a time- and decision-specific capacity assessment, presumption of capacity, practical support, and recording the assessment; UK legal rules require UAE adaptation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: When mental illness, self-harm or leaving risk is relevant, the exact RCEM section supports recording immediate safety, capacity for the decision to leave, specialist discussion and handover only when assessed or arranged.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.

unresolved_source_gaps: Specific care declined, information provided, alternatives, comprehension, capacity elements, legal authority, surrogate, immediate risk, departure circumstances and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
